questions.question: Solve for x: |x − 5| = 12
questions.explanation: Split into two cases: x − 5 = 12 gives x = 17, and x − 5 = −12 gives x = −7. Check both in the original equation.

questions.question: A temperature sensor reads within 2°F of the actual temperature. If the sensor reads 68°F, write and solve an absolute value equation to find the range of actual temperatures.
questions.explanation: The absolute value equation |T − 68| = 2 gives T = 70 or T = 66. The actual temperature is any value in the interval [66, 70].

questions.question: Solve the inequality: |x + 4| < 3
questions.explanation: For |A| < B, use −B < A < B. So −3 < x + 4 < 3. Subtract 4: −7 < x < −1.

questions.question: Solve: |3x − 6| = |x + 2|
questions.explanation: When two absolute values are equal, either 3x − 6 = x + 2 (giving x = 4) or 3x − 6 = −(x + 2) (giving 4x = 4, so x = 1). Verify both solutions.

questions.question: Solve the inequality: |2x − 5| ≥ 9
questions.explanation: For |A| ≥ B, use A ≥ B or A ≤ −B. So 2x − 5 ≥ 9 gives x ≥ 7, and 2x − 5 ≤ −9 gives x ≤ −2.
